title: Quantitative Estimation of Auxin, Siderophore, and Hydrogen Cyanide Production in Bacteria for Cucumber Growth
author: Zeinab Fotoohiyan
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML

steps.title: Halotolerant PGPR bacterial strains Note
steps.instruction: Halotolerant PGPR bacterial strains Note: Our strains were isolated from field soil samples at a depth of 30 cm in the arid and saline regions of Jiroft (Kanarsandal, Jazsaleh, Anbarabad, and Karimabad areas) in Kerman province, Iran. All isolates were found to belong to the Bacillus genus, with isolate C8 identified as the most effective. Following PCR amplification of the 16S rDNA, an approximate 1,500 bp band was observed on an agarose gel. Amplicon sequencing was conducted by Bioneer Corporation in South Korea. After sequencing the amplified products, BLAST alignment was performed on NCBI to find a strain with a high degree of homology. The molecular weight of these sequence fragments matched that of the bacterial 16S rDNA fragment. Based on the BLAST results and the phylogenetic tree analysis, the C8 isolate was identified as Bacillus subtilis, as its amplicon sequence shared 99%–100% similarity with Bacillus subtilis. Bacterial codes: K: Kanarsandal; C: Karimabad; J: Jazsaleh; and A: Anbarabad.

steps.title: Collect soil samples from a depth of 30 cm. Note
steps.instruction: Collect soil samples from a depth of 30 cm. Note: We collected soil from the dry and saline areas of Jiroft County farms (Kanarsandal, JozeSaleh, Anbarabad, and Karimabad) in Kerman Province, Iran, during the 2020 agricultural year.

steps.title: Transfer samples to the laboratory in sterile plastic bags
steps.instruction: Transfer samples to the laboratory in sterile plastic bags.

steps.title: Add 10 g of each soil...
steps.instruction: Add 10 g of each soil sample to vials containing 90 mL of sterile distilled water.

steps.title: Shake the mixture at 100 rpm...
steps.instruction: Shake the mixture at 100 rpm and 30 °C for 1 h.

steps.title: Prepare serial dilutions of the soil...
steps.instruction: Prepare serial dilutions of the soil suspensions up to 10 -5 .

steps.title: Prepare minimal agar medium
steps.instruction: Prepare minimal agar medium.

steps.title: Prepare TSA medium in a slant...
steps.instruction: Prepare TSA medium in a slant form in screw-cap test tubes.

steps.title: Perform spread plating using a glass...
steps.instruction: Perform spread plating using a glass rod, adding 60 μL of each dilution to the minimal agar medium.

steps.title: Incubate the samples at 30 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples at 30 °C in the dark for 48 h. Randomly select five isolates per rhizosphere soil sample and re-streak them on TSA plates for colony purification.

steps.title: Culture all purified isolates on TSA...
steps.instruction: Culture all purified isolates on TSA in test tubes to prepare stock cultures.

steps.title: Incubate for 24 h at 30 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 24 h at 30 °C.

steps.title: Store the isolates in a refrigerator at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Store the isolates in a refrigerator at 4 °C. B. Screening of salt-tolerant bacteria

steps.title: Prepare nutrient broth liquid medium
steps.instruction: Prepare nutrient broth liquid medium.

steps.title: Add sodium chloride in varying concentrations...
steps.instruction: Add sodium chloride in varying concentrations of 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 35, and 40 g to create salinity levels of 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 25%, 35%, and 40%, respectively. Adjust the total volume in each flask to 100 mL with nutrient broth.

steps.title: Incubate the samples for 24 h at 30 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples for 24 h at 30 °C.

steps.title: Measure the growth of the isolates...
steps.instruction: Measure the growth of the isolates using a spectrophotometer, with optical density readings taken at 660 nm.

steps.title: Select 11 salt-tolerant isolates capable of...
steps.instruction: Select 11 salt-tolerant isolates capable of growing in a medium containing 40% sodium chloride. C. Screening of drought-tolerant bacteria

steps.title: Create water potentials of 0
steps.instruction: Create water potentials of 0.50, -0.15, -0.30, -0.49, and -0.73 MPa by adding calculated amounts of polyethylene glycol 6000 to each liter of nutrient broth medium, following the formula below [19]: Water potential (wp) = –(1018e-2)c – (1.18e-4)c + (2.67e-4)ct + (8.39e-7)cT Where: T = temperature in Kelvin c = polyethylene glycol concentration t = ambient temperature e = constant coefficient wp = water potential

steps.title: Add a 0
steps.instruction: Add a 0.1 mL sample of liquid culture containing salt-tolerant bacteria to each of the different water potential treatments, with three replicates for each treatment.

steps.title: Incubate the vials at 30 ±...
steps.instruction: Incubate the vials at 30 ± 2 °C on a shaker set to 120 rpm for 24 h.

steps.title: Assess bacterial growth by measuring optical...
steps.instruction: Assess bacterial growth by measuring optical density at 660 nm using a spectrophotometer.

steps.title: Prepare control samples without the addition of polyethylene glycol
steps.instruction: Prepare control samples without the addition of polyethylene glycol. D. Quantitative estimation of auxin

steps.title: Estimate auxin levels using the Brick method [20]
steps.instruction: Estimate auxin levels using the Brick method [20].

steps.title: Prepare a nutrient broth medium containing 0
steps.instruction: Prepare a nutrient broth medium containing 0.5% L-tryptophan (500 μg/mL) and 2% sodium chloride

steps.title: Culture the isolates in this medium ( Figure 1
steps.instruction: Culture the isolates in this medium ( Figure 1 .1).

steps.title: Incubate the cultures in a shaking...
steps.instruction: Incubate the cultures in a shaking incubator at 27 ± 2 °C for 48 h ( Figure 1 .2).

steps.title: After incubation, centrifuge the samples for...
steps.instruction: After incubation, centrifuge the samples for 10 min at 11,200rpm ( Figure 1 .3).

steps.title: Prepare Salkowski reagent (see Recipe 9)
steps.instruction: Prepare Salkowski reagent (see Recipe 9).

steps.title: Mixed 1 mL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Mixed 1 mL of the supernatant with 1 mL of Salkowski reagent in a test tube ( Figure 1 .4).

steps.title: Incubate the reaction mixture at 27...
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction mixture at 27 ± 2 °C for 20 min in the dark.

steps.title: The presence of IAA is indicated...
steps.instruction: The presence of IAA is indicated by a pinkish-red color in the test tube ( Figure 1 .5–6).

steps.title: Measure the optical absorption at 530 nm ( Figure 1
steps.instruction: Measure the optical absorption at 530 nm ( Figure 1 .7).

steps.title: Estimate the amount of IAA using...
steps.instruction: Estimate the amount of IAA using a standard IAA curve (Hi-media) and expressing in μg/mL [21]. Figure

steps.title: Quantitative estimation of auxin production in...
steps.instruction: Quantitative estimation of auxin production in halotolerant bacterial isolates for cucumber growth.

steps.title: Inoculate isolates in nutrient broth medium containing 0
steps.instruction: Inoculate isolates in nutrient broth medium containing 0.5% L-tryptophan.

steps.title: Incubate for 48 h on a rotary shaker
steps.instruction: Incubate for 48 h on a rotary shaker.

steps.title: Centrifuge bacterial cultures at 11,200× g for 10 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge bacterial cultures at 11,200× g for 10 min.

steps.title: Mix 1 mL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Mix 1 mL of the supernatant with 1 mL of Salkowski’s reagent.

steps.title: Keep in the dark for 20 min
steps.instruction: Keep in the dark for 20 min.

steps.title: A reddish-pink color indicates the production of IAA
steps.instruction: A reddish-pink color indicates the production of IAA.

steps.title: Measure absorbance at 530 nm
steps.instruction: Measure absorbance at 530 nm. E. Measurement of siderophore production Note: Siderophore production was assessed using the CAS shuttle assay method ( Castaneda et al., 2005 ), which is based on the color change of CAS from blue to orange due to iron removal by siderophores.

steps.title: Culture selected isolates in iron-minimal medium...
steps.instruction: Culture selected isolates in iron-minimal medium at 30 °C for 48 h ( Figure 2 .1).

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 81,600× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 81,600× g for 15 min ( Figure 2 .2).

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL of CAS reagent to each supernatant from the isolate cultures ( Figure 2 .3).

steps.title: Incubate the samples at 30 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples at 30 °C for 20 min ( Figure 2 .4).

steps.title: Iron removal in the presence of...
steps.instruction: Iron removal in the presence of siderophores results in a color change from blue to orange ( Figure 2 .5).

steps.title: Measure color intensity using a spectrophotometer...
steps.instruction: Measure color intensity using a spectrophotometer at a wavelength of 630 nm ( Figure 2 .6).

steps.title: Calculate siderophore production as a percentage...
steps.instruction: Calculate siderophore production as a percentage using the following formula [22]: Siderophore production (%) = {(Ar - As) × 100}/Ar Where: Ar = reference absorbance (CAS solution + uninoculated medium) As = sample absorbance (CAS solution + cell-free supernatant of the sample) Figure

steps.title: Quantitative estimation of siderophore production in...
steps.instruction: Quantitative estimation of siderophore production in halotolerant bacterial isolates for cucumber growth.

steps.title: Culture selected isolates in minimum iron medium at 30 °C
steps.instruction: Culture selected isolates in minimum iron medium at 30 °C.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples for 15 min at 81,600× g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples for 15 min at 81,600× g.

steps.title: Add CAS solution to the supernatant
steps.instruction: Add CAS solution to the supernatant.

steps.title: Incubate at 30 °C for 20 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 30 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: Observe a decrease in the intensity of the blue color
steps.instruction: Observe a decrease in the intensity of the blue color.

steps.title: Measure the color intensity at 630 nm
steps.instruction: Measure the color intensity at 630 nm. F. Measurement of hydrogen cyanide (HCN) production

steps.title: Prepare nutrient broth medium containing 4
steps.instruction: Prepare nutrient broth medium containing 4.4% glycine ( Figure 3 .1).

steps.title: Prepare 3 mL of bacterial suspension...
steps.instruction: Prepare 3 mL of bacterial suspension (10 6 CFU/mL) from each isolate.

steps.title: Inoculate bacteria into a 250 mL...
steps.instruction: Inoculate bacteria into a 250 mL flask containing 100 mL of the prepared medium at 30 °C for 48 h.

steps.title: Soak a Whatman No
steps.instruction: Soak a Whatman No. 1 filter paper in a solution of 2% sodium carbonate and 0.5% picric acid and place it over the surface of the culture medium ( Figure 3 .2–3).

steps.title: Inoculate the samples at 28 ±...
steps.instruction: Inoculate the samples at 28 ± 2 °C for 96 h ( Figure 3 .4).

steps.title: Evaluate HCN production by a color...
steps.instruction: Evaluate HCN production by a color change of the filter paper from yellow to orange-brown ( Figure 3 .5–6). Figure

steps.title: Quantitative estimation of hydrogen cyanide production...
steps.instruction: Quantitative estimation of hydrogen cyanide production in halotolerant bacterial isolates for cucumber growth.

steps.title: Grow bacterial isolates in nutrient broth containing 4
steps.instruction: Grow bacterial isolates in nutrient broth containing 4.4% glycine.

steps.title: Soak filter papers in a solution...
steps.instruction: Soak filter papers in a solution of 2% sodium carbonate and 0.5% picric acid.

steps.title: Place the soaked filter papers on...
steps.instruction: Place the soaked filter papers on the surface of the culture medium.

steps.title: Incubate at 28 ± 2 °C for 96 h
steps.instruction: Incubate at 28 ± 2 °C for 96 h.

steps.title: Observe for a distinct color change
steps.instruction: Observe for a distinct color change.

steps.title: Note the shift from yellow to orange-brown
steps.instruction: Note the shift from yellow to orange-brown. G. Investigation of insoluble phosphate solubilization

steps.title: Prepare solid Picosky medium ( Figure 4
steps.instruction: Prepare solid Picosky medium ( Figure 4 .1).

steps.title: Inoculate isolates onto solid Picosky medium...
steps.instruction: Inoculate isolates onto solid Picosky medium and incubate at 30 ± 2 °C for five days ( Figure 4 .2).

steps.title: Assess phosphate solubilization by observing a...
steps.instruction: Assess phosphate solubilization by observing a clear halo around the colony ( Figure 4 .3).

steps.title: Inoculate phosphate-solubilizing isolates into liquid Picosky...
steps.instruction: Inoculate phosphate-solubilizing isolates into liquid Picosky medium and shake at 3 rpm for 7 days at 30 ± 2 °C ( Figure 4 .4–5).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16,100× g for 10...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16,100× g for 10 min at 25 °C to remove bacterial cells and solid particles ( Figure 4 .6).

steps.title: Transfer the clear supernatant from each vial into new vials
steps.instruction: Transfer the clear supernatant from each vial into new vials.

steps.title: Measure absorbance at 820 nm using...
steps.instruction: Measure absorbance at 820 nm using a spectrophotometer ( Figure 4 .7).

steps.title: Conduct the quantification of solubilized phosphorus...
steps.instruction: Conduct the quantification of solubilized phosphorus by comparing it with a standard KH 2 PO 4 curve [23].

steps.title: Select four salt-tolerant isolates with the...
steps.instruction: Select four salt-tolerant isolates with the best growth-promoting characteristics for greenhouse studies. Figure

steps.title: Quantitative estimation of insoluble phosphate solubilization...
steps.instruction: Quantitative estimation of insoluble phosphate solubilization in halotolerant bacterial isolates for cucumber growth.

steps.title: Culture the bacterial isolates on Picosky solid medium
steps.instruction: Culture the bacterial isolates on Picosky solid medium.

steps.title: Incubate at 30 ± 2 °C for five days
steps.instruction: Incubate at 30 ± 2 °C for five days.

steps.title: Monitor the formation of clear zones around the bacterial colonies
steps.instruction: Monitor the formation of clear zones around the bacterial colonies.

steps.title: Inoculate the isolates into Picosky liquid medium
steps.instruction: Inoculate the isolates into Picosky liquid medium.

steps.title: Incubate for 7 days on a...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 7 days on a shaker at 150 rpm and 30 ± 2 °C.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 10 min at 16,100×...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 min at 16,100× g and 25 °C to remove bacterial cells and solid particles.

steps.title: Measure absorbance at 820 nm
steps.instruction: Measure absorbance at 820 nm. H. Greenhouse studies

steps.title: Prepare Royal cultivar cucumber seeds
steps.instruction: Prepare Royal cultivar cucumber seeds.

steps.title: The seeds undergo surface sterilization for...
steps.instruction: The seeds undergo surface sterilization for 3 min in a 5% sodium hypochlorite solution, followed by 8–10 rinses with sterile distilled water. Treat them for 3 min with the fungicide captain (Wp50%).

steps.title: Wash the seeds three times with...
steps.instruction: Wash the seeds three times with sterile distilled water for 3 min each time.

steps.title: Select four superior isolates (K4, K14, K15, and C
steps.instruction: Select four superior isolates (K4, K14, K15, and C

steps.title: for evaluation of their growth-promoting potential
steps.instruction: for evaluation of their growth-promoting potential.

steps.title: Plant eight pre-germinated seeds in each...
steps.instruction: Plant eight pre-germinated seeds in each clean pot (21 × 16 ×16 cm) at a depth of 2 cm. Maintain at 28 °C, with 75% relative humidity and a 12/12 h photoperiod ( Figure 5 .1–2).

steps.title: Inoculate each seed with 3 mL...
steps.instruction: Inoculate each seed with 3 mL of a bacterial suspension containing 10 8 cells per mL ( Figure 5 .3).

steps.title: Arrange the greenhouse experiment as a...
steps.instruction: Arrange the greenhouse experiment as a factorial based on a completely randomized design with two factors: salinity at four levels (control, 4, 8, and 16 dS/m, achieved by adding NaCl to the irrigation water) and five bacterial treatments (no inoculation and separate inoculations with four salt-tolerant bacteria), with three replications. Maintain soil moisture at 70% field capacity.

steps.title: Six weeks after planting, measure vegetative...
steps.instruction: Six weeks after planting, measure vegetative parameters (plant height, fresh and dry weight, and leaf area) ( Figure 5 .4).

steps.title: Measure seedling height using a graduated...
steps.instruction: Measure seedling height using a graduated ruler with millimeter precision ( Figure 5 .5).

steps.title: Measure fresh weight using a digital scale accurate to 0
steps.instruction: Measure fresh weight using a digital scale accurate to 0.001 g ( Figure 5 .5).

steps.title: Measure dry weight using a digital...
steps.instruction: Measure dry weight using a digital scale after drying samples in an oven for 48 h at 72 °C ( Figure 5 .5).

steps.title: Measure leaf area using a leaf...
steps.instruction: Measure leaf area using a leaf scanner and Photoshop graphic software ( Figure 5 .5). Figure

steps.title: Plant growth-promoting ability of the four superior isolates
steps.instruction: Plant growth-promoting ability of the four superior isolates.

steps.title: Surface-sterilize the cucumber seeds
steps.instruction: Surface-sterilize the cucumber seeds.

steps.title: Plant the seeds in pots at...
steps.instruction: Plant the seeds in pots at a depth of 2 cm.

steps.title: Add 3 mL of bacterial suspension to each seed
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL of bacterial suspension to each seed.

steps.title: Grow the plants for six weeks
steps.instruction: Grow the plants for six weeks.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol or a part of it has been used and validated in the following research articles: Barazani and Friedman. [20]. Effect of exogenously applied l-tryptophan on allelochemical activity of plant growth promoting rhizobacteria (PGPR). Journal of Chemical Ecology. Castaneda et al. [25]. A spectrophotometric method to determine the siderophore production by strains of Pseudomonas fluorescent in the presence of copper and iron. Microchemical Journal. Castric. [26]. Hydrogen cyanide, a secondary metabolite of Pseudomonas aeruginosa . Canadian Journal of Microbiology. Pikovskaya. [27]. Mobilization of phosphorus in soil in connection with vital activity of some microbial species. Microbiologyal.
